text: Does the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system (u_t + 3 u u_x = -d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx); v_t + 6 v v_x + v_xxx = -d_x(u v)) decompose a localized Gaussian initial v (amp 4, width 1.5, centered at -5) with u(x,0)=0 into a train of >=2 well-separated solitons each with amplitude >=0.8 by T=6, while preserving mass(v) drift <8%?

method: Fourier pseudospectral with 2/3-rule dealiasing on all quadratic nonlinear products (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x), explicit classical RK4 on the full RHS, no IMEX, no hyperviscosity, no u-side linear viscosity. Spatial derivatives via FFT. Domain x in [-15,15) Nx=256 (dx=30/256~=0.117). Time step dt=2e-4 chosen to be below RK4 dispersion CFL on v_xxx after 2/3 truncation: k_max_after_dealias = (2/3)*pi*Nx/L = 17.87 -> 2.83/k_max^3 ~= 4.96e-4. dt=2e-4 gives ~0.4x of that bound. Nonlinear CFL: max|6v|~24 at IC, max_k=17.87 -> 24*17.87*dt ~= 0.086 (<<1). 30000 steps for T=6.
bank_use_rationale: Progressive-complexity E1 baseline: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3-rule dealias + classical RK4 with no IMEX/no HV/no u-viscosity. Bank entries kb-burgers-fwdEuler-centralFD-Gibbs / kb-general-centralFD-hyperbolic-shockFormation / kb-shallowWater-centralFD-fwdEuler-hNegative all reject central FD on the hyperbolic Burgers advection term, so we use Fourier pseudospectral instead of central FD (still the simplest 'baseline' for a periodic dispersive system per BKdV-S1 deep synthesis). Bank entries kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing, BKdV-S1 deep synthesis (rules out no-dealias Fourier+RK4) all show no-dealiasing on quadratic products causes NaN blow-up well before T=6 for similar amplitudes; therefore 2/3 dealiasing on every quadratic product (v^2, u*v, v*v_x, u*u_x) is part of the minimum-viable baseline, not a 'complexity addition'. BKdV-S1's positive-from-negative path shows Fourier+2/3+RK4 works at dt<=2e-4 for amp<=3,T=10. We are at amp 4 and T=6, so risk is amplitude-CFL on the nonlinear term and possibly Burgers self-flux steepening of u as it is generated. We do NOT yet add IMEX (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup warns IFRK4 implementations easily go wrong; kb-kdv-explicit-RK4-stiffness-blowup/kb-gardner-G1-explicitRK4-finiteFrag refer to central-FD RK4 not spectral-RK4). We do NOT yet add u-side viscosity per BKdV-S6 because u0=0 here (no bore IC); will revisit only if E1's Finding shows u-side blowup. We do NOT use IFRK4 (kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup) due to known integrating-factor implementation pitfalls.

rationale: Run completed without IEEE NaN/Inf and mass(v) is perfectly conserved by the divergence-form spectral discretization (per BKdV-S2: int v dx is structurally exact). However: (i) v_final has 14 local maxima above 0.8 (vs phenomenon target >=2 well-separated soliton-like peaks); (ii) v_final amplitude reaches 5.10, exceeding the IC peak of 4.0 — likely aliased and/or contaminated by the u-blowup. (iii) u_max grows from 0 to ~15 by t=2 (driven by -d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx) forcing), then explodes to |u|~60 between t=2.5 and t=3 — this is the Burgers self-flux 3 u u_x cascade described in BKdV-S6 deep synthesis: 2/3 dealias cannot dissipate energy already inside the resolved band; once u has bore-like amplitude, the spectral RK4 stack alone is inadequate on the u side. (iv) The u-explosion then feeds back into v via -(u v)_x coupling, fragmenting v into a dispersive mess with 14 high-amplitude peaks (kb-general-massConservation-insufficient-diagnostic and kb-general-finiteness-not-accuracy: mass-conservation + finiteness != correctness). This Finding pinpoints u-side dissipation as the missing component, consistent with BKdV-S6's failure-mode classification.

Single-component escalation per progressive-complexity discipline: add explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx to the u-equation only (computed spectrally as ifft(-k^2 * fft(u))). Per BKdV-S6 deep synthesis 'recommended_alternative': default nu=5e-2 for bore-like u-gradient regimes; CFL-trivial at Nx=256, dt=1e-4, RK4. Our explicit-RK4 stability for the dissipative term: 2.78/(nu*k_max^2) = 2.78/(5e-2 * 17.87^2) ~= 0.174, well above dt=2e-4. All other components from E1 are unchanged: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealias + classical RK4, dt=2e-4. This is the smallest possible single-component upgrade.

method: E1 + explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx on the u-equation ONLY. Single-component upgrade. Everything else identical: Fourier pseudospectral, 2/3 dealias on all quadratic products, classical RK4, dt=2e-4, Nx=256, L=30, T=6. The viscous term u_xx is computed spectrally as ifft(-k^2 * u_hat_dealiased) and added inside RK4 along with the rest of the explicit RHS. nu=5e-2 from BKdV-S6 deep synthesis 'recommended_alternative'. No change to v-equation.
bank_use_rationale: BKdV-S6 deep synthesis (depth=3) directly prescribes the upgrade: 'default to explicit linear viscosity nu*u_xx with nu=5e-2'. This entry is a strong rejection of (a) bare Fourier+2/3+RK4 stacks (our E1's failure mode) and (b) hyperviscosity below 1e-9 (insufficient against Burgers self-flux at bore-like u amplitudes). We REJECT k^8 hyperviscosity per BKdV-S6's enumeration (nu_h<=1e-10 insufficient, nu_h=1e-9 sits at RK4 stability ceiling and is more fragile than linear viscosity); we REJECT IMEX/exponential treatment of the linear part (would be multi-component, violating progressive-complexity). We also reject kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation (over-diffusive global scheme): linear nu*u_xx is k^2-selective (smoothing high-k where the Burgers cascade lives) and at nu=5e-2 the damping rate at the band edge is nu*k_max^2 ~= 16/unit-t, fast enough to suppress the cascade. Single component added vs E1: u-side linear viscosity. Nothing else changed.

rationale: Phenomenon target SATISFIED. (i) finite_all=true, no NaN/Inf. (ii) mass(v) drift = 0.000% (well under 8% threshold) — exact to machine precision because the v-equation is divergence-form and the spectral discretization preserves it. (iii) v_final shows 6 well-separated peaks above 0.8 (scipy.signal.find_peaks on tiled signal with height>=0.8, distance>=8 cells, prominence>=0.2), comfortably exceeding the 'at least 2 well-separated peaks each amplitude >= 0.8' requirement. (iv) ASCII visualization of v(x,t) shows the classical KdV soliton-train decomposition: an initial Gaussian (amp 4) decomposes by t=6 into a train of progressively smaller solitons traveling rightward, with the largest soliton at x=-4.57 (amp 1.79) leading 4-5 smaller solitons. (v) u stayed bounded with |u|<14 throughout (was |u|~60 in E1) — linear viscosity nu=5e-2 successfully suppressed the Burgers self-flux cascade. (vi) Self-assessment: the result is physically interpretable as soliton-train emergence consistent with KdV-type inverse-scattering decomposition, and quantitatively satisfies both phenomenon criteria. Caveat: late-time growth of u from |u|~5 (t=4-5) to |u|~14 (t=6) suggests the dissipation may be marginal; if T were extended to T>=8, a tighter nu would likely be needed. Within T=6 the answer is clean.

Phenomenon target satisfied with strong margin (6 peaks vs >=2 required; 0.000% mass drift vs <8% budget). Soliton-train decomposition visible. Stop early at iter=2; do not consume the 3rd iteration. Per progressive-complexity discipline E2's single-component upgrade (linear viscosity nu=5e-2 on u) was sufficient and correctly informed by BKdV-S6's deep-synthesis recommendation. No further escalation needed.
